steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The final sentence is the key signal: Villasana sees both approaches as ways of depicting the power and interconnectedness of all people. The main idea must capture both the diversity of her subjects and their unified purpose.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Villasana's works focus on recognizing both famous and everyday examples of human strength and connection.: matches exactly — both subject types, both themes. ✓
  • Choice B — Villasana began her artistic career by painting portraits of famous people and then transitioned to depicting everyday people instead.: invents a timeline the passage never mentions. ✗
  • Choice C — Villasana's depiction of Nina Simone receives more attention from scholars than her depiction of a woman making tortillas does.: introduces critical response the passage doesn't discuss. ✗
  • Choice D — Villasana's portrayal of a woman making tortillas focuses more on human connection than it does on human resilience.: misreads the passage, which contrasts two bodies of work, not elements within one piece. ✗
That is the correct answer.
